metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the missing `docs/your-first-change.md` edit target: either remove it from the Scope IN clause and AC3 (link only from CONTRIBUTING.md, which covers the full onboarding flow), or explicitly add creation of `docs/your-first-change.md` to the IN scope with a corresponding AC item.
findings.finding: `docs/your-first-change.md` does not exist in the repository, but the plan lists it as an edit target in Scope IN ('links from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md') and in AC3 ('Linked from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md'). The file is absent from `docs/` and from a repo-wide search, so AC3 as written cannot be satisfied without either creating the file (unscoped work) or silently skipping it.
findings.location: Scope IN clause; Acceptance Criteria AC3: 'Linked from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'links from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md' with 'links from CONTRIBUTING.md' in the Scope IN clause and update AC3 accordingly. CONTRIBUTING.md already contains comprehensive contributor onboarding and a link there is sufficient.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the missing `docs/your-first-change.md` edit target: either remove it from scope (only link from CONTRIBUTING.md) or explicitly add its creation to the IN scope and add a corresponding AC item.
findings.finding: docs/your-first-change.md does not exist in the repository, but the plan names it as an edit target that must be updated to link to the new review-policy.md. Attempting to add a link to a non-existent file will either fail or require creating that file — neither of which is in scope.
findings.location: Scope section: 'IN: docs/review-policy.md (new), links from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'links from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md' with 'links from CONTRIBUTING.md' in the Scope IN clause and update the corresponding AC item, since CONTRIBUTING.md already contains the full contributor onboarding content and a link there is sufficient.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the docs/your-first-change.md reference: either add that file to the Scope's IN list as a new file to create (with a one-sentence stub that links to review-policy.md), or replace the AC's second link target with an existing file (e.g. CONTRIBUTING.md §2c or docs/README.md) that actually exists today.
findings.finding: The plan's Acceptance Criteria requires the new doc to be 'Linked from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md', but docs/your-first-change.md does not exist in the repository (no matches in the repo). The Scope section lists only docs/review-policy.md as a new file IN scope. An implementer following the plan as written will either silently create an out-of-scope file or leave a broken AC, because you cannot link FROM a file that does not exist.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Linked from CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md'; Scope — IN section
findings.suggested_fix: Either add `docs/your-first-change.md (new stub, links to review-policy.md)` to the Scope IN section, or replace 'docs/your-first-change.md' in the AC and Scope with a currently-existing file such as `docs/README.md` or remove it if CONTRIBUTING.md alone is sufficient.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line requiring that `docs/review-policy.md` contains a sentence explicitly distinguishing the code-review gate from the plan-review gate and linking to `docs/plan-review-gate.md`.
findings.finding: The 'one clarifying sentence distinguishing the code-review gate from the plan-review gate, with a link to docs/plan-review-gate.md' is a named deliverable in the What section but has no corresponding acceptance criterion to verify it was actually written.
findings.location: What section (final bullet); Acceptance Criteria (all four AC lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: `- [ ] \`docs/review-policy.md\` contains a sentence explicitly distinguishing the code-review merge gate from the plan-review (ticket) gate, with a link to \`docs/plan-review-gate.md\`.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the 'exact commands: `make lint && make typecheck && make test`' formula with an accurate summary of the Verified gate's full check set (or link to gerrit-verify.yaml as the authoritative source rather than enumerating commands inline).
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the exact commands: `make lint && make typecheck && make test`' for the Verified/CI gate, but reading `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` shows the Verified workflow runs materially more: require-ticket (rebar ticket trailer check), module-size gate, prompt-index drift gate, security-rules freshness gate, criteria-routing parity gate, public-types drift gate, `make config-check`, pip-audit, and both unit AND integration pytest tiers — none of which appear in the three-command formula. Publishing the incorrect 'exact commands' in the review policy will mislead contributors about what CI actually gates on.
findings.location: What section: 'the exact commands: `make lint && make typecheck && make test`; link the workflow'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the description to: 'Verified (CI — runs the full gate suite from `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`: lint, typecheck, tests, config-check, commit-ticket validation, dependency audit, and several drift/parity gates; link the workflow for the canonical list).' Avoid listing specific commands inline since the workflow is the authoritative source.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the coverage-gap sub-reason enumeration in the vote-semantics table to include all values in `_TAG_SUFFIXES` (`indeterminate`) and the merge-path tag (`merge-review`), so the published doc is complete with respect to the live bot's actual output vocabulary.
findings.finding: The plan's vote-semantics table enumerates the coverage-gap sub-reasons as 'llm-unavailable / scanner / gate-disabled / review-error', but the authoritative _TAG_SUFFIXES enum in src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py also defines 'indeterminate', and src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py line 160 emits a distinct 'coverage-gap (merge-review)' tag for merge-path failures. The two unlisted values will appear in real bot messages that the published policy doc never explains, leaving contributors confused about an infra veto they cannot map to any documented category.
findings.location: What section — vote-semantics table spec: 'coverage-gap (llm-unavailable / scanner / gate-disabled / review-error)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan's sub-reason list from 'llm-unavailable / scanner / gate-disabled / review-error' to 'llm-unavailable / scanner / gate-disabled / review-error / indeterminate / merge-review', and add a row or footnote in the vote-semantics table for each: 'indeterminate' = LLM returned non-PASS with no blocking findings (could not establish coverage — infra, not your code); 'merge-review' = merge-path REST failure during merge-change review.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the plan for the separate-doc choice over expanding CONTRIBUTING.md (e.g. 'CONTRIBUTING.md is a procedural how-to; review-policy.md is the normative authority/dispute/responsibility layer that can be linked from ADRs and the README independently') and specify whether review-policy.md should reference CONTRIBUTING.md §2c for the vote-semantics table rather than duplicating it.
findings.finding: The plan states the chosen shape of a separate docs/review-policy.md is 'convergent' (citing Chromium/Zuul) but does not provide a positive rationale for why a separate doc is preferable to expanding CONTRIBUTING.md §2c, which already contains the vote-semantics table, coverage-gap distinction, and no-self-approval fact in nearly identical form. Without this rationale, an implementer has no guidance on how to bound overlap between the two docs or which is authoritative when they diverge over time.
findings.location: What section — framing decision paragraph; Why section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a response-time expectation (or explicit 'best-effort, target N days') and a fallback (e.g., 'if no maintainer response within 5 business days, the submitter may merge with the dispute comment as the audit trail') to the dispute-path description in docs/review-policy.md.
findings.finding: The dispute path ('resolve the comment thread with written justification, or raise it to the maintainer') does not specify any response-time commitment or fallback if the maintainer is unresponsive, leaving an external contributor potentially blocked indefinitely with no recourse described.
findings.location: What section — 'Bot vs human authority' / dispute path bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Bot vs human authority' section, after 'raise it to the maintainer', add: 'The maintainer aims to respond within [N] business days. If a dispute remains unresolved after that window, the submitter may request an admin merge, which will be logged in the change's audit trail.' This preserves the override escape hatch while giving contributors a concrete exit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified prior-art assertions: either supply a verifiable citation (URL / KEP / document name) for the 'Kubernetes 2026 AI-review policy' reference, or explicitly scope the novelty framing to the 'Why' section of the plan (internal rationale only) and instruct the doc not to carry the unverified claim.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote' and specifically names the 'Kubernetes ecosystem's 2026 AI-review policy' as advisory-only contrast, but neither claim carries a verifiable citation or URL. These are specific factual assertions about external artifacts; if the published doc inherits this framing (or even if only the plan carries it), a contributor or reviewer who tries to verify them has no anchor. The Kubernetes policy reference is especially load-bearing: it is the primary prior-art comparator used to justify the gate's novelty and the 'required-with-human-override' framing decision.
findings.location: Why section — 'research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote' and 'Kubernetes ecosystem's 2026 AI-review policy makes AI review advisory-only'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a citation anchor to the Kubernetes reference in the plan (e.g., the KEP number or a direct URL to the policy document). If the reference cannot be verified, replace the specific claim with a hedged form ('As of 2026, no widely-adopted OSS project was found to document a *blocking* LLM merge vote — advisory-only AI review is the current norm') and mark it as internal rationale, not doc content.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `docs/plan-review-gate.md` already exists (or add its creation to scope), OR replace the link with a stub anchor/TODO comment and a note that the plan-review gate doc is forthcoming.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the new doc to link to `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, but that file is neither confirmed to exist nor included in this plan's scope — creating a broken reference on day one.
findings.location: What section: 'Distinguish explicitly: … link to docs/plan-review-gate.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (1) verify the file exists and add its path to the Acceptance Criteria, or (2) change the sentence to 'the plan-review gate is a separate internal-workflow gate (doc forthcoming)' with no live link until the file is created.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `docs/plan-review-gate.md` exists (or will be created as a prerequisite) before merging this plan; if it does not exist yet, replace the link with a TODO comment or a stub file so review-policy.md ships without a broken reference.
findings.finding: The plan directs review-policy.md to include a link to `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, but plan-review-gate docs are explicitly marked OUT OF SCOPE for this plan. If that file does not yet exist when review-policy.md is published, the link will be broken — a cross-document dependency introduced without confirming the target is present.
findings.location: What section ('Distinguish explicitly … link to docs/plan-review-gate.md') and Scope section ('OUT: … the plan-review gate's own docs')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite line to the Scope or AC: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md must exist (even as a stub) before this change merges.' Alternatively, change the inline reference to 'docs/plan-review-gate.md (forthcoming)' until that doc is published.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
